name: ChatGPT
tagline: A leading AI assistant for writing, coding, research, analysis, brainstorming, and everyday productivity
description: ChatGPT is an AI assistant from OpenAI for writing, coding, research, document analysis, image understanding, voice conversations, and business productivity.
users: Creators, students, developers, educators, researchers, and teams
bestFor: Writing, coding, research, business, and education
overview: ChatGPT is a conversational AI assistant created by OpenAI. It lets people ask questions, draft content, analyze files, generate code, summarize information, brainstorm ideas, and complete complex knowledge-work tasks through a natural chat interface. Instead of learning a rigid software workflow, users describe what they want in plain language and refine the answer through follow-up questions. That combination of accessibility and depth is why ChatGPT has become one of the most recognizable AI tools for individuals, classrooms, startups, and enterprise teams.

At its core, ChatGPT is built to understand context and produce useful responses across many formats. A marketer can ask it to turn rough notes into a campaign brief. A developer can request a function, debug an error, or compare implementation approaches. A teacher can create a lesson outline and adapt it for different grade levels. A business owner can upload a spreadsheet or policy draft and ask for trends, risks, or next steps. The experience feels less like using a traditional app and more like collaborating with a fast research, writing, and analysis partner.

People use ChatGPT because it reduces friction in everyday work. It can help with first drafts, improve clarity, explain difficult concepts, translate between languages, generate ideas, create tables, outline presentations, and analyze documents. With image understanding and voice conversations, it also supports multimodal workflows such as interpreting screenshots, reviewing visual materials, or talking through a problem hands-free. For many users, the biggest productivity gain is not replacing expert judgment; it is getting from a blank page to a useful starting point much faster.

The tool is especially popular in education, software development, marketing, sales, operations, customer support, research, and entrepreneurship. Real-world examples include drafting customer emails, building a study guide, creating a resume summary, troubleshooting code, comparing vendors, preparing interview questions, summarizing meeting notes, planning social media calendars, and pressure-testing a business idea. ChatGPT is strongest when the user provides context, reviews the output carefully, and treats the assistant as a collaborative tool rather than an unquestioned source of truth. For users who want one flexible AI platform that can handle many daily tasks, ChatGPT remains one of the best overall options in 2026.

features.title: Natural conversations
features.description: Ask questions, refine answers, and build on context without learning complex commands.

features.title: Code generation
features.description: Generate functions, explain errors, review snippets, and compare technical approaches.

features.title: Document analysis
features.description: Summarize, restructure, and extract insights from uploaded documents and long text.

features.title: Image understanding
features.description: Interpret screenshots, diagrams, charts, photos, and other visual materials.

features.title: Voice conversations
features.description: Talk through ideas, practice explanations, or get hands-free assistance.

features.title: Deep Research
features.description: Use deeper research workflows for more thorough answers and source-aware summaries.

features.title: Custom GPTs
features.description: Create specialized assistants for repeat workflows, brands, teams, or knowledge areas.

features.title: File uploads
features.description: Work with PDFs, spreadsheets, notes, briefs, and other files in a single conversation.

features.title: Data analysis
features.description: Explore datasets, generate charts, identify patterns, and explain findings.

features.title: API ecosystem
features.description: Developers can build AI features into apps using OpenAI's platform and models.

features.title: Prompt memory
features.description: Personalization can help ChatGPT adapt to preferences and recurring instructions.

features.title: Project organization
features.description: Keep related chats, files, and context together for ongoing workstreams.

pros: Excellent all-around assistant for writing, coding, research, and planning
Very easy to start using, even for non-technical users
Strong multimodal support for text, files, images, and voice
Useful paid tiers for power users and teams
Large ecosystem of Custom GPTs, workflows, and API integrations
Helpful for both quick tasks and deeper project-based work
cons: Important outputs still require human fact-checking
Best advanced features may require a paid plan
Not always the best dedicated search tool for current-event research
Teams with strict data requirements should review privacy and admin settings
Highly specialized tasks may be better served by vertical AI tools

notFor.title: Users who need guaranteed facts without review
notFor.description: ChatGPT can be extremely helpful, but it should not replace verification for legal, medical, financial, or high-stakes decisions. Perplexity or Google may be better for source-first browsing.

notFor.title: Teams requiring only a dedicated writing style tool
notFor.description: If your only need is brand-governed marketing copy, a specialized writing platform or Notion AI workflow may be a cleaner fit.

notFor.title: Organizations locked into another ecosystem
notFor.description: Microsoft-heavy teams may prefer Copilot, Google Workspace teams may prefer Gemini, and users who prioritize long-form reflective writing may also compare specialized assistant.
